tldr: Cerbos is a general authorization engine, Turnkey is secure-enclave wallet/key infrastructure — moving between them implies a product pivot, not a feature swap.
why_switch: Your product is becoming a crypto/fintech app needing key custody
You want secure-enclave signing with programmable policies
You no longer need a general PDP for authorization
You prefer non-custodial key management exposed via API
You are consolidating around wallet primitives instead of authz primitives
what_to_export: Custom data sources or context providers
export_method: Cerbos policies are file-based — keep your policies directory under version control. Export Cerbos decision logs through its audit exporter as described in the Cerbos official docs.
import_method: Turnkey is wallet/key infrastructure, not an authz engine. Model users, sub-organizations, and signing policies in Turnkey per the Turnkey official docs; reimplement any non-signing authorization in your application or in a dedicated authz layer.

concept_mapping.note: Verify entity model in current docs

gotchas: Turnkey does not replace generic application authorization
Turnkey pricing is not fully transparent — get a quote first
Plan key recovery, root user, and policy emergency-break procedures
Run signing flows in a sandbox before going live
Audit how non-signing access decisions get enforced after Cerbos is removed

email.subject: Cancellation: Cerbos Cloud / commercial plan
email.body:
Hi Cerbos team,

We are pivoting toward wallet and key infrastructure and adopting Turnkey, so we will be winding down our Cerbos commercial usage. Please confirm the cancellation steps, final invoice timing, and any export we should run before account closure.

Thanks for the work on Cerbos.

Best regards
